description_wiki: Hingham is a market town and civil parish in mid-Norfolk, England.

surface_forms: pretty well scattered over New England
aliases: New England Region of the United States
description: region of the northeasternmost United States
description_wiki: New England is a region consisting of six states in the Northeastern United States: Connecticut, Maine, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, Rhode Island, and Vermont.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: New Hampshire ( HAMP-shər) is a state in the New England region of the Northeastern United States.

description: revolution establishing the United States of America
description_wiki: The American Revolution (1765–1783) was a political movement in the Thirteen Colonies of Great Britain and the United States which the colonies founded.

description_wiki: Georgia (  JOR-jə) is a state in the Southeastern, South Atlantic, and Deep South regions of the United States.

description: territory of the United States of America from 1798 to 1817
description_wiki: The Territory of Mississippi was an organized incorporated territory of the United States that was created under an organic act passed by the Congress of the United States.

description: city in Henry County, Alabama, USA
description_wiki: Abbeville /ˈæbɪvɪl/ is a city in and the county seat of Henry County, in the southeast part of Alabama, United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Oklahoma (  OH-klə-HOH-mə; Choctaw: Oklahumma, pronounced [oklahómma]) is a landlocked state in the South Central and Southwestern region of the United States.

description: defunct automotive manufacturer
consolidated city-county in Bibb County, Georgia, United States of America
description_wiki: Macon ( MAY-kən), officially Macon–Bibb County, is a consolidated city-county in Georgia, United States.

description: Native American people indigenous to the Southeastern United States
description_wiki: The Cherokee ( CHAIR-ə-kee,   CHAIR-ə-KEE; Cherokee: ᎠᏂᏴᏫᏯᎢ, romanized: Aniyvwiyaʔi / Anigiduwagi, or ᏣᎳᎩ, Tsalagi) people are one of the Indigenous peoples of the Southeastern Woodlands of the United States.

description: conflict between the United States and the British Empire from 1812 to 1815
description_wiki: The War of 1812 was fought by the United States and its allies against the United Kingdom and its allies in North America.

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: Dunavant is a surname.

description: colonial empire governed by Spain between 1492 and 1976
description_wiki: The Spanish Empire, sometimes referred to as the Hispanic Monarchy or the Catholic Monarchy, was a colonial empire that existed between 1492 and 1976.

description: citizens or residents of The Netherlands
description_wiki: The Dutch (Dutch: ), are an ethnic group native to the Netherlands.

description: army officer who betrayed America to the British during the Revolutionary War (1740–1801)
description_wiki: Benedict Arnold (January 14, 1741 [O.S. January 3, 1740] – June 14, 1801) was an American-born British military officer who served during the American Revolutionary War.

description: city in Alabama, United States of America
description_wiki: Elba is a city in and the county seat of Coffee County, Alabama, United States.

aliases: The University of Alabama at Birmingham
description: public university in Birmingham, Alabama
description_wiki: The University of Alabama at Birmingham (UAB) is a public research university in Birmingham, Alabama, United States.

description: American jurist and politician (1899–1985)
description_wiki: John Jackson Sparkman (December 20, 1899 – November 16, 1985) was an American jurist and politician from the state of Alabama.

description: county seat of Mobile County, Alabama, United States
description_wiki: Mobile ( moh-BEEL, French: [mɔbil] ) is a city and the county seat of Mobile County, Alabama, United States.

description: largest city of the state of Louisiana, United States
description_wiki: New Orleans (commonly known as NOLA or  The Big Easy, among other nicknames) is a consolidated city-parish located along the Mississippi River in the U.S. state of Louisiana.

description: severe economic crisis in the USA in the 1930s
description_wiki: In the United States, the Great Depression is commonly (though not universally) dated to the Wall Street crash of October 1929.

description: city in and county seat of Montgomery County, and capital of the State of Alabama, United States
description_wiki: Montgomery is the capital city of the U.S. state of Alabama and the seat of Montgomery County.

description: county in Alabama, United States
description_wiki: Cullman County is a county located in the north central portion of the U.S. state of Alabama.

description: city in Marshall County, Alabama, United States
description_wiki: Guntersville (previously known as Gunter's Ferry and later Gunter's Landing) is a city and the county seat of Marshall County, Alabama, United States.

aliases: United States Federal Civil Works Administration
description: New Deal-era job creation program implemented by the U.S. federal government
description_wiki: The Civil Works Administration (CWA) was a short-lived job creation program established by the New Deal during the Great Depression in the United States in order to rapidly create mostly manual-labor jobs for millions of unemployed workers.

description: United States government New Deal agency (1935–1939) employing millions of unemployed people to carry out public works projects
description_wiki: The Works Progress Administration (WPA; from 1935 to 1939, then known as the Work Projects Administration from 1939 to 1943) was an American New Deal agency that employed millions of jobseekers (mostly men who were not formally educated) to carry out public works projects, including the construction of public buildings and roads.

aliases: War Food Administration Office of Production
description: New Deal agency to combat rural poverty during the Great Depression in the United States
description_wiki: The Farm Security Administration (FSA) was a New Deal agency created in 1937 to combat rural poverty during the Great Depression in the United States.

description: capital city of the United States of America
description_wiki: Washington, D.C., officially the District of Columbia and commonly known as simply Washington or D.C., is the capital city and federal district of the United States.

description: private university in Washington, D.C., USA
description_wiki: The George Washington University (GW or GWU) is a private federally chartered research university in Washington, D.C., United States.

description: president of the United States from 1953 to 1961 (1890–1969)
description_wiki: Dwight David "Ike" Eisenhower (born David Dwight Eisenhower; October 14, 1890 – March 28, 1969) was the 34th president of the United States, serving from 1953 to 1961.

description: United States Army general in WWI, WWII and Korea (1880–1964)
description_wiki: Douglas MacArthur (26 January 1880 – 5 April 1964) was an American general who served as a top commander during World War II and the Korean War, achieving the rank of General of the Army.

description: archipelagic country in Southeast Asia
description_wiki: The Philippines, officially the Republic of the Philippines, is an archipelagic country in Southeast Asia.
